sanskrit:
यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः।
निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्॥
transliteration:
yad agre cānubandhe ca sukhaṁ mohanam ātmanaḥ |
nidrālasyapramādotthaṁ tat tāmasam udāhṛtam ||
literal_meaning: That happiness which at the outset and in the aftermath deludes the self, arising from sleep, laziness and heedlessness, is declared to be of the mode of tamas (ignorance).
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the pseudo-pleasure that numbs self-awareness: a dopaminergic fog of comfort-seeking that masks underlying dread and keeps the person stuck in inertia.
psychological_state: Learned helplessness and behavioral shutdown; the psyche defends against overwhelm by retreating into torpor, mistaking stupor for safety.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media in bed for hours, feeling neither joy nor pain, just a heavy fog that makes showering and answering emails feel impossible.
anchor_text: Comfort that dulls awareness is the false bliss of tamas.
